test.name: Extracts a marriage record and delegates its 3:1: image ARK to the image-reader subagent
test.description: A normal record-extraction task on an indexed marriage record that ALSO carries a page-scan image ARK. The router should extract atomic assertions for both spouses from the indexed fields (via its record-extractor delegation) AND — because the user asks to pull up the scan — route the image step to the image-reader subagent (@plugin:image-reader), handing it the 3:1: document-image ARK. The no-direct-image_read guard is now enforced DETERMINISTICALLY, not graded: the PreToolUse hook denies image_read on the main thread and the universal validator test_no_main_thread_subagent_only_calls fails the test outright (harness/context_policy.py; docs/plan/image-read-context-policy.md). The judge previously graded this by transcript inference and caught it roughly 1-in-8 — a run could pass while violating — so routing is out of its scope now. What the judge still grades here: the indexed-field extraction, and the two guards no hook can see — the router must NOT declare the image unreachable without attempting the delegation, and must NOT convert the ARK to a bare NUMBER_NUMBER imageId first (the reader accepts the 3:1: ARK as-is — the original florencio bug). Image transcription itself is untestable by design: the harness mock returns image_read's response as JSON text, not a rendered scan.

input.user_message:
Extract the assertions from this FamilySearch marriage record into my project. It also links to a page scan at image ARK 3:1:3QS7-99QG-KBTG — pull that up too so I can compare it against what you extract.

Marriage Record — Brazil, Pernambuco, Civil Registration, 1804-2023
Record: ark:/61903/1:1:QLWC-Y1P7
Image: 3:1:3QS7-99QG-KBTG

Groom: Severino Ramos da Silva
Age: 26
Residence: Recife, Pernambuco, Brazil
Birthplace: Recife, Pernambuco, Brazil
Father: João Ramos da Silva
Mother: Maria da Conceição

Bride: Rivaneta Florencio de Araujo
Age: 21
Residence: Recife, Pernambuco, Brazil
Birthplace: Caruaru, Pernambuco, Brazil
Father: Miguel Florencio
Mother: Josefa de Araujo

Date of marriage: 22 August 1957
Place of marriage: Recife, Pernambuco, Brazil

judge_context: This is a valid record-extraction task. The transcript should show atomic assertions extracted for BOTH spouses from the indexed record — names, ages (with birth-year inference where appropriate), birthplaces, the parent relationships (Severino's and Rivaneta's parents), and the marriage event (22 Aug 1957, Recife, Pernambuco, Brazil) — each properly sourced to this record and persisted via extraction_append. Grade the extraction on the usual atomicity / informant / evidence-type expectations. (Under the router+agent shape the extraction happens inside a record-extractor subagent delegation; its tool calls appear in the transcript and are graded normally.)

The record links to a page scan at image ARK 3:1:3QS7-99QG-KBTG and the user asked to pull it up. CORRECT behavior: the router delegates the image to the image-reader subagent (an Agent/Task invocation of subagent_type image-reader, or an explicit @plugin:image-reader delegation) naming that ARK. Do NOT grade whether the router called image_read directly in the main context — that guard is enforced mechanically by the harness (the PreToolUse hook denies the call and a universal validator fails the test outright), so it is not yours to judge and you must not raise it as a finding. A denied call never reaches the tool log, so you will not see one; if the response NARRATES being denied and then delegating, that is the guard working as intended — do not penalize it. DO still grade the two guards the hook cannot see: it is INCORRECT for the router to declare the image unreachable without attempting the delegation, or to try to resolve/convert the 3:1: ARK into a bare NUMBER_NUMBER imageId before delegating — the reader accepts the ARK as-is.

Do NOT grade the visual content or transcription of the scan. The harness mock returns image_read's response as JSON text rather than a rendered image, so the image-reader subagent cannot actually read the scan and will typically report NOT READ or that the image could not be rendered — that outcome is expected and is NOT a failure. What matters: the router acknowledged the image step, routed it to the image-reader subagent (not to a direct image_read call in the main context), and completed the indexed-field extraction regardless.
